title: Soy wax jar candle with rose geranium
subtitle: A 200 ml container candle with rose geranium essential oil
excerpt: A soy wax container candle scented with rose geranium essential oil at 6% of wax weight. A softer, more herbal alternative to a pure rose fragrance. Burns approximately 30-35 hours.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry candle-making guides (brambleberry.com, accessed May 2026): essential oil versus fragrance oil in soy wax candles. CandleScience (candlescience.com, accessed May 2026): wick sizing for standard jar candles.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 x 200 ml jar candle (~30-35 burn hours)
recipe.batchNotes: Four jars from 600 g wax and 36 g rose geranium EO is a comfortable batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure 24-48 hours before the first burn. Essential oil throw in soy wax is softer than fragrance oil; keep the room small for best effect.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Effectively indefinite unlit. Essential oil scent fades faster than synthetic fragrance oil; burn within 18 months for the best throw. Wax does not go off.

glossaryTerms.definition: The first burn sets the candle's burn pattern permanently. Burn until the wax pool reaches the jar edge before extinguishing. For a 7 cm jar this takes 3-4 hours.

The wax temperature at the moment of pouring. For container soy wax, 55-65°C. Essential oils are more volatile than fragrance oils; add them at the lower end of this range (55°C) to reduce evaporation during pouring.

The temperature at which a fragrance material produces enough vapour to ignite. Rose geranium essential oil has a flash point around 78°C. Adding it to wax above 65°C risks volatilising the top notes before the candle is poured.

content.text: Rose geranium essential oil (Pelargonium roseum) produces a softer, greener fragrance than a rose absolute, with herbal and slightly sweet undertones. In a soy wax candle it gives a gentler hot throw than synthetic fragrance oils, which suits a small room or bedroom. Using an essential oil rather than a fragrance oil keeps the scent closer to the plant and avoids any synthetic base notes.
The key difference from a fragrance-oil candle is the
flash point

. Rose geranium EO has a lower flash point than most candle-grade fragrance oils, so add it at the lower end of the pour temperature window (55°C rather than 65°C). Stir gently rather than rapidly, and pour promptly.
Melt wax over a simmering-water bath, not directly on the hob. Never leave melting wax unattended. Keep a fire blanket nearby.
What you need

items.prepNote: 6% of wax weight. Pelargonium roseum. Add at 55°C, at the lower end of the pour window, to preserve the top notes.
CD-12 or equivalent for a 7 cm jar.

content.text: Method
Set the wick
Stick the wick tab to the clean, dry jar base. Thread the wick through the centring bar and position across the jar top to keep it straight and centred.
Melt the wax
Weigh the soy wax into the candle pouring pitcher and melt over a simmering-water bath. Allow to cool to 55°C, checking with the probe thermometer.
Add essential oil and pour
At 55°C, add the rose geranium essential oil. The
pour temperature
is lower for essential oils to preserve the volatile top notes. Stir gently for 2 minutes, then pour slowly into the prepared jar. Leave undisturbed at room temperature to set.
Cure and first burn
Allow 24 hours to set before the first burn. Trim the wick to 5 mm. The
memory burn
must reach the jar edge before extinguishing; allow 3-4 hours for the first burn. Trim the wick before every subsequent burn to prevent smoking.
Storage and shelf life

Store in a cool, dark place away from sunlight. The soy wax does not go off. The rose geranium essential oil scent fades faster than synthetic fragrance oil; burn within 18 months for the best throw. After that the candle will still burn cleanly but with a reduced scent.
